title: Python Candlestick Recognition Engine (II) — Detection Using Ta-Lib
subtitle: 60+ Pattern Detection via TA-Lib CDL Functions, Signed Return Codes, Priority Hierarchy
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: MEDIUM EXTRACT — ta-lib CDL function pattern (already available in our Python env), signed return codes for direction, priority hierarchy (Engulfing > Harami > Doji). Key: 60+ patterns for no manual coding.

core_concept.ta_lib_pattern: import talib; result = talib.CDLENGULFING(open, high, low, close); non-zero = pattern present; positive = bullish, negative = bearish
core_concept.priority_hierarchy: When multiple patterns detected at same bar: Engulfing > Harami > Doji
core_concept.60_plus_patterns: CDLHAMMER, CDLSHOOTINGSTAR, CDLDOJI, CDLENGULFING, CDLHARAMI, CDLMORNINGSTAR, CDLEVENINGSTAR, CDLPIERCING, CDLDARKCLOUDCOVER, CDLABANDONBABY, etc.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: ta-lib is already available. One-line pattern detection replaces our manual classifier. But ta-lib default thresholds may need calibration for each instrument — our ATR-normalized checks are more robust for synthetics.

ta_lib_usage.batch: patterns = {name: getattr(talib, name)(o,h,l,c) for name in ['CDLHAMMER','CDLENGULFING','CDLMORNINGSTAR',...]}

config.HARAMI_TOL: Tolerance for manual Harami refinement override

keep: ta-lib CDL function batch detection pattern
Signed return codes (positive=bullish, negative=bearish) for clean direction output
60+ patterns available — no manual coding needed
improve: Use ta-lib as backend for classify_candle() but add ATR-normalized body filters on top (ta-lib ignores body size vs ATR)
Run ta-lib patterns as supplemental quality signal: if ta-lib detects Morning Star at zone → highest confidence departure
